expectations.text: Flags that the SEO agent calling the new agent directly violates hub-and-spoke orchestration (Principle 38)
expectations.evidence: 'Problem 1' quotes Principle 38 and Principle 4; states the SEO agent calling SitemapAgent makes a spoke coordinate another spoke.

expectations.text: Recommends coordination flow through the orchestrator rather than direct agent-to-agent calls (Principle 4)
expectations.evidence: Line 17: the determination flows back to the orchestrator, which decides to dispatch; recommended shape step 4 forbids spoke-to-spoke calls.

expectations.text: Flags that deterministic sitemap-building should not live in an agent reasoning loop and belongs in a service or tool (Principles 2 and 6)
expectations.evidence: 'Problem 2': deterministic crawl + XML belongs in a service (thick core, P2) with a thin tool (P6/P7); 'the reasoning loop is the part to drop.'

expectations.text: Raises skill-first extension — a skill, tool, or service should be considered before a new agent (Principle 41)
expectations.evidence: Problem 2 quotes Principle 41 verbatim and applies the lowest-power-layer ladder; recommended shape step 5 adds a skill only if real instructional nuance exists.

expectations.text: Concludes that the proposed shape is off and gives a concrete recommended alternative shape
expectations.evidence: Concludes the shape cuts against how the Kite agentic system is built and provides a numbered 'Recommended shape'.

expectations.text: Grounds the critique in the appsmith-v2 principles catalogue and cites specific principle numbers
expectations.evidence: Cites P38, P4, P41, P6, P2, P9, P39, P34, P37 and quotes the catalogue.
